title: pelargonium × actinogramma
intro: Pelargonium × actinogramma, also known as Mountain Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Geraniaceae family, known for its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds like geraniol, linalool, and limonene, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × actinogramma have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and sore throats, as well as to support digestion and menstrual health. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap the health benefits of this versatile plant.
